name: Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular from Jaraba Sanctuary
name_original: Pinturas rupestres de la Roca Benedí y Cascada de Calmarza — circular desde el Santuario de Jaraba
region_intro: Hiking around Jaraba offers diverse natural landscapes, including dramatic canyons, impressive waterfalls, and unique geological formations. The region is characterized by its significant rock walls, serene lakes, and varied forests of pinyon, juniper, oak, and pine. Hikers can explore areas with sculpted red rock formations and historical sites partially embedded in the rock. This terrain provides a range of experiences from challenging ascents to more gentle loops. Best hiking trails around Jaraba The most popular hiking route is Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular from Jaraba Sanctuary, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and leads to ancient rock paintings and a waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pozo Redondo Waterfall – Santuario de Jaraba loop from Jaraba, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.7 km) path. This trail leads past the Pozo Redondo Waterfall and the historic Santuario de Jaraba, offering varied scenery. Local hikers also love the Santuario de Jaraba – Shepherds' Shelters loop from Jaraba, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail exploring the area around the Santuario de Jaraba and shepherds' shelters, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Jaraba is defined by canyons, waterfalls, and rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Jaraba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Jaraba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Jaraba?
A: Jaraba is renowned for its diverse natural landscapes, including dramatic canyons like the Barranco de la Hoz Seca with its enormous rock walls, impressive waterfalls such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caprichosa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and serene lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5119833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirror Lake (Lago del Espejo)&lt;/a&gt;. You'll also encounter unique geological formations, sculpted red rock formations, and varied forests of pinyon, juniper, oak, and pine.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls in Jaraba?
A: Yes, Jaraba offers several opportunities to experience stunning waterfalls. The region is close to the famous Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park, home to numerous cascades including the impressive Cola de Caballo. For a local route, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Redondo Waterfall – Santuario de Jaraba loop from Jaraba&lt;/a&gt;, which leads past the picturesque Pozo Redondo Waterfall.

Q: Can I find historical or cultural landmarks along the hiking trails in Jaraba?
A: Absolutely. The area is rich in history. The Barranco de la Hoz Seca features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Jaraba&lt;/a&gt;, partially embedded in the rock. You can also explore ancient rock paintings on the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974346145/rock-paintings-of-roca-benedi-and-calmarza-waterfall-circular-from-jaraba-sanctuary" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular from Jaraba Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; route. Additionally, the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra Monastery Ruins&lt;/a&gt; offer a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Jaraba?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Jaraba are designed as circular routes, perfect for day trips. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34337199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario de Jaraba – Shepherds' Shelters loop from Jaraba&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.4 km loop exploring the area around the Sanctuary. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shepherds' Shelters loop from El Sabinar&lt;/a&gt;, a shorter 5.2 km moderate route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Jaraba?
A: Jaraba offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. While winter offers a different, quieter beauty, some trails might be affected by colder weather, so checking local conditions is advisable.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Jaraba?
A: The hiking trails in Jaraba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse scenery, from dramatic canyons and waterfalls to serene forests and unique rock formations. The blend of natural beauty and historical sites, like the Sanctuary of Jaraba, also receives positive feedback.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Jaraba?
A: While specific 'easy' routes are not highlighted in the data, many of the moderate loops around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Jaraba&lt;/a&gt; or the Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park (which has a well-marked 5 km trail) can be suitable for families. These trails offer varied scenery without extreme difficulty, allowing for a leisurely pace.

Q: Is Jaraba a good destination for challenging hikes?
A: Yes, Jaraba caters to more experienced hikers looking for a challenge. The region features routes with significant elevation gain and varied terrain. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974346145/rock-paintings-of-roca-benedi-and-calmarza-waterfall-circular-from-jaraba-sanctuary" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular from Jaraba Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, a 16.1 km trail that takes over 5 hours to complete and leads to ancient rock paintings and a waterfall.

Q: Are there options for longer hikes in Jaraba?
A: For those seeking longer excursions, Jaraba offers several routes that extend beyond a few hours. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974346145/rock-paintings-of-roca-benedi-and-calmarza-waterfall-circular-from-jaraba-sanctuary" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular from Jaraba Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 16.1 km trail. Other moderate options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Redondo Waterfall – Santuario de Jaraba loop from Jaraba&lt;/a&gt; (11.7 km) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shepherds' Shelters – Santuario de Jaraba loop from Jaraba&lt;/a&gt; (11.9 km) also provide extended hiking experiences.

Q: What kind of terrain will I encounter on Jaraba's hiking trails?
A: The terrain in Jaraba is quite varied, reflecting its diverse natural landscapes. You can expect paths through pinyon, juniper, oak, and pine forests, alongside dramatic canyons with significant rock walls, and areas featuring sculpted red rock formations. Some routes involve elevation changes, while others offer more gentle loops.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or viewpoints along the trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, hikers can discover several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Jaraba&lt;/a&gt; is a unique religious building partially embedded in rock. The Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park, easily accessible from Jaraba, offers numerous viewpoints of its famous waterfalls and lakes, including the iconic Cola de Caballo and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5119833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirror Lake (Lago del Espejo)&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
The Sanctuary of the Virgin of Jaraba is a place of great historical and spiritual interest, located in the stunning setting of the Barranco de la Hoz Seca. Built in the 18th century, the sanctuary is known for its architecture and natural setting.
It is said that the Virgin of Jaraba appeared on a rock oozing oil, giving it a miraculous quality.

The Roca Benedí cave paintings are one of the westernmost examples of Levantine rock art on the Iberian Peninsula. They are located within the Barranco de la Hoz Seca, near Jaraba, in the province of Zaragoza.
The paintings depict a hunting scene with two human figures (a man and a woman) and two animals, painted in black.
This is an essential corner for nature lovers. The "Pozo Redondo" waterfall in Calmarza is a charming place that is hidden in the lower part of the town where the Mesa River passes.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgin of Jaraba
